codeReview.summary: Clean implementation. The admin stats route replaces three static chart imports with React.lazy() wrappers that correctly map named exports through .then(mod => ({ default: mod.ComponentName })), and wraps the chart grid in a single Suspense boundary with loading placeholders. The named export targets match the source modules, the change stays within the requested file, and the worker handoff reports successful build and lint verification. SSR compatibility is acceptable here because the Remix server entry uses renderToPipeableStream, which supports Suspense streaming.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The codebase has two valid lazy-loading patterns, but the distinction is undocumented. pdf-viewer-lazy.tsx uses an isClient guard before rendering a lazy component, while admin stats uses bare React.lazy() + Suspense on the route. This feature is correct because recharts does not have the browser-only worker constraint that pdf-viewer has, but AGENTS.md does not explain when a client-only guard is required versus when standard React.lazy() is sufficient.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/remix/app/components/general/pdf-viewer/pdf-viewer-lazy.tsx:14,17,29 uses lazy() with an isClient guard; apps/remix/app/routes/_authenticated+/admin+/stats.tsx:37,43,49,179 uses lazy() and Suspense without one; apps/remix/app/entry.server.tsx:42 renders with renderToPipeableStream.

summary: PASS. The feature correctly lazy-loads the three recharts chart components on the admin stats page with a Suspense fallback, satisfies VAL-BUNDLE-002, and no blocking or non-blocking code issues were found.
